title: Colin Cowherd: Celtics future feels ‘weird’ compared to sustainable Knicks
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Colin Cowherd claims Celtics' heavy reliance on Tatum and Brown's contracts limits their long-term flexibility.
Cowherd argues Knicks have better salary cap flexibility thanks to Jalen Brunson's team-friendly contract.
Knicks advance further in playoffs than Celtics, playing in NBA Finals against San Antonio Spurs.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Hoppers', a new Pixar film, is streaming on Disney+ featuring a teen's adventure in the animal kingdom.
'Pillion', a film exploring a unique romance, is available on HBO Max without a traditional rating.
Apple TV's new series 'Cape Fear', based on John D. MacDonald’s novel, stars Amy Adams and Javier Bardem.

title: Red Sox move Garrett Crochet to 60-day IL as ace continues to deal with discomfort
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Garrett Crochet moved to the 60-day injured list by the Red Sox, extending his absence until at least June 25.
The roster move allows Boston to add newly acquired reliever Joe La Sorsa to their 40-man roster.
Brayan Bello struggles and is demoted to Triple-A as Boston's rotation compensates for Crochet's absence.

title: Endicott baseball falls to Denison in hard-fought DIII  National Championship Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Endicott College's baseball team narrowly lost to Denison University 4-3 in Game 3 of the DIII National Championship.
The Gulls forced the decisive Game 3 with an 11-10 walk-off win in Game 2 of the series.
Endicott baseball made history as the first varsity team from the school to reach a national title game.

title: Laxative-laced brownies rattle Nantucket School Committee meeting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Nantucket resident Meghan Perry presented laxative-laced brownies at a School Committee meeting, sparking controversy.
The brownie stunt protested a proposal for artificial turf at Nantucket High School due to PFAS concerns.
Police filed a report on the incident; no one ate the brownies, and potential charges are unclear.

title: Former McDonald’s manager accused of spitting in ex-girlfriend’s fries arraigned on felony charge
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kaylie Santos, a former Southbridge McDonald's manager, arraigned for spitting in ex-girlfriend's fries, faces one felony count.
Santos, 22, pleaded not guilty to distributing food with harmful substance, a charge carrying up to five years in prison.
Viral Snapchat video and surveillance footage allegedly show Santos contaminating and serving the fries on April 9.

title: How many players of Giannis Antetokounmpo’s caliber have been traded?
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo, with seven All-NBA first-team selections, is among a rare group possibly being traded.
Only five players with as many All-NBA first-team selections as Antetokounmpo have ever been traded.
The Celtics are reportedly a 'wild card' team in potential trade talks for Giannis Antetokounmpo.

title: Review & setlist: In Boston, St. Vincent goes symphonic to stunning effect
author: Mary Nordmann
bullets: St. Vincent performed with the Boston Pops at Symphony Hall on June 4, 2026, enhancing her music with a 60-piece orchestra.
Conductor Jules Buckley collaborated with St. Vincent to transform her songs for the performance, including tracks from her album MASSEDUCTION.
St. Vincent expressed gratitude to Boston, referencing her time as a Berklee student, and engaged the audience throughout the concert.

title: 5 storylines to follow as the Red Sox and Yankees battle in the Bronx
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: The Red Sox aim to improve their record against Yankees in a crucial three-game series in the Bronx.
Ben Rice, hitting .300 this season, seeks to improve his performance against the Red Sox's pitching.
Yankees' Cam Schlittler, with a 1.89 ERA, remains a formidable opponent for the struggling Red Sox lineup.

title: Canton, Norfolk DA issue statements on texts included in Karen Read’s new lawsuit
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Sgt. Sean Goode resigned from Canton Police amid an internal investigation into inappropriate text messages linked to Karen Read's lawsuit.
Karen Read's lawsuit includes vulgar messages allegedly sent by former officers Proctor and Goode, prompting responses from Canton and Norfolk DA's Office.
The Norfolk DA continues to review cases involving former Trooper Proctor to ensure fair prosecution following his offensive text revelations.
